research_profile.style_preference: 中英双语，中文在前，英文在后；克制、具体、有判断力，避免新闻稿式写作

topic_threads.summary: FutureWorld frames live future prediction as a delayed-feedback RL environment for agents, suggesting a shift from static benchmarks toward real-world outcome rewards. Key concern: unresolved outcomes and delayed ground truth can create missing-reward bias.
Lifting Embodied World Models emphasizes controllable high-level action abstractions over raw prediction quality. Waypoint-conditioned lifting may be a useful lens for agent-world-model integration.
reward-lens extends mechanistic interpretability to scalar reward models. The main finding to carry forward is that observational attribution and causal patching can disagree sharply.

EnterpriseDocBench evaluates document AI as a multi-stage pipeline across parsing, indexing, retrieval, and generation. Early evidence questions simple linear cascade assumptions but remains limited by pilot data and automated metrics.
DataClaw shows a chat-native data agent design with ReAct, local execution, persistent memory, pluggable skills, and event streaming. Current evidence is demo-level rather than benchmark-level.

open_questions: How should live agent training environments handle delayed, missing, or disputed real-world outcomes without introducing reward-selection bias?
For world-model agents, should evaluation prioritize high-level controllability and planning efficiency alongside prediction fidelity?
Can reward-model interpretability be integrated during reward-model training rather than only after training?
What benchmark design best captures enterprise document workflows rather than only structural document complexity?
What end-to-end metrics should data agents report: task success, auditability, privacy, local execution cost, memory quality, or all of these?

recent_digests.next_recall: Prioritize papers that test live feedback loops, world-model action abstraction, reward-model causal interpretability, document-pipeline evaluation, and data-agent benchmarks.
